task: Stop Codex manual handoff tasks from returning to running after task_complete
verification: Observed ai_task 4e63512c-3611-4e25-ae8f-675ffa8dfb52 had status=running while rollout thread 019ea7ec-76e9-7110-afa3-940aa2c860dd contained task_complete and visible Codex reply
Restarted focusmap-agent; ai_task 4e63512c-3611-4e25-ae8f-675ffa8dfb52 changed to awaiting_approval with codex_run_state=awaiting_approval
why: The bug was inside one local monitor state machine. Splitting would not help because the fix is the shared definition of running vs awaiting_approval from rollout evidence.
mistakes: Codex thread updated_at_ms was treated as resume evidence after task_complete. Because task_complete.completed_at is second precision while thread updated_at_ms is millisecond precision, completed Codex replies could be reclassified as running.
next_routing_rule: For Codex thread status bugs, treat task_complete/turn_aborted as stronger evidence than thread updated_at_ms. Only user_message or task_started after the checkpoint may resume an awaiting task.

next_routing_rule: For Codex monitor interval changes, keep local sqlite/rollout checks, Turso heartbeat/snapshot write dedupe, Supabase target-list read cadence, and docs/CONTEXT.md in the same change.

task: Investigate and strengthen Focusmap Mac app login persistence and first-pass external browser auth handoff
why: The login failure crossed one shared Mac auth handoff contract: Electron pending nonce tracking, external browser callback, local session persistence, and logout cleanup. Splitting would risk making the callback and local restore paths diverge.
mistakes: Mac app auth persistence relied on the login page saving sessions, so a dashboard reached via existing cookies could remain logged in while auth-session.json stayed missing. The external-browser OAuth handoff also relied only on process-local memory, which can be flaky across production instances.
next_routing_rule: For Mac app authentication bugs, inspect the Electron profile, auth-session.json, callback handoff path, and logout cleanup together. Do not rely only on server process memory for native app auth handoff.
